paper_title: Radiocarbon Dating and Robustness Reasoning in Archaeology

methods.description: This method involves the collection and processing of archaeological samples for radiocarbon dating, ensuring minimal contamination and accurate measurement of radiocarbon ratios.

steps.description: Establish archaeological field practices for recovering and handling samples that minimize contamination by younger or older organic material.
steps.searchable_summary: develop field practices to minimize sample contamination

steps.description: Refine methods by which radiocarbon laboratories measure 14C in archaeological samples.
Standardize protocols for the recovery and handling of datable samples.
steps.searchable_summary: standardize sample recovery and handling protocols

steps.description: Refine laboratory techniques to control for electromagnetic impurities, ambient radiation, radon contamination, and fractionation.
steps.searchable_summary: control for impurities and contamination in lab techniques

methods.description: This method involves using robustness reasoning and multiple determination strategies to ensure that temporal data accurately track cultural phenomena and are not distorted by extraction and measurement processes.

steps.description: Apply strategies of multiple determination to ensure temporal data track cultural phenomena accurately.
steps.searchable_summary: apply multiple determination strategies to temporal data

steps.description: Ensure source data and warrants backing data claims are well substantiated.
Require causal and conceptual independence between various types of temporal data used to calibrate one another.
steps.searchable_summary: ensure causal and conceptual independence in data calibration
